channel.title: BinderBrief Daily
channel.description: Daily commercial insurance and underwriting automation insights.

item.title: New KYC Tool From Trustd -
item.description:
Trustd has launched Know Your Carrier (KYC), a web-based tool providing instant risk assessments on carriers via email with inputs like email address and VAT number. The tool verifies email legitimacy, matches domain and business registration data, scans for adverse media, sanctions, court judgments, and uses Trustd’s network intelligence, integrating into existing workflows without installation. This tool aims to reduce cargo theft risks linked to carrier impersonation, improving underwriting accuracy and lowering loss exposure for commercial brokers and carriers in European freight markets.
Name: Lyall Cresswell, Company: Trustd, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO and Founder of TEG and Trustd, expert in freight-matching infrastructure and digital identity verification.

item.title: How broken interactions are costing insurers growth
item.description:
Insurer Earnix identified a persistent gap between advanced back-office systems and customer-facing interactions despite widespread digital transformation investments over the past decade. This gap arises because key decision-making data—pricing logic, underwriting rules, and policy details—rarely integrates seamlessly into customer engagement channels, leading to delays and inconsistent responses during customer interactions. Closing this gap by embedding decisioning capabilities directly in customer workflows can improve underwriting efficiency, reduce operational costs, and enhance broker margins by reducing customer churn and improving retention.
Name: N/A, Company: Earnix, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: A technology provider specializing in insurance pricing and underwriting decision systems

item.title: D&O Market Expected to Tighten Under Pressure, Says AM Best
item.description:
Analysts at AM Best reported that the Directors & Officers (D&O) liability insurance market faced its fourth consecutive year of premium declines in 2025, with a direct loss ratio rising to 54.5% and reserve inadequacies noted for 2023 and 2024 accident years. The report highlights slower claims closure rates, increased underwriting risk due to geopolitical and regulatory factors, and the need for insurers to perform detailed due diligence on industry sectors while managing emerging risks such as AI disclosure and ESG compliance. These developments indicate pressure on underwriting margins, a tightening market capacity, and reduced broker flexibility in providing competitive D&O insurance products in 2026.
Name: David Blades, Company: AM Best, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Associate director at the insurance rating firm AM Best who provided analysis on the D&O liability market trends Name: Chad Hemenway, Company: Carrier Management, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article reporting on the AM Best D&O liability market analysis

item.title: Register: Risky Future AI Tools for Retail Agents 'Demo Day' on June 10
item.description:
Insurance Journal’s Risky Future is hosting an AI Tools for Retail Agents Demo Day on June 10, featuring companies that provide AI solutions for retail insurance agencies. The event demonstrates AI applications such as workflow automation, customer service tools, cross-sell gap identification, and AMS integrations designed to improve sales, servicing, and renewal processes. These AI tools aim to increase agency efficiency, reduce manual work time, and potentially improve broker margins and carrier underwriting operations by automating routine tasks.
Name: Libertate Representative, Company: Libertate, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Develops AI customer-facing agents for carriers, agents, MGAs, and insurance operations. Name: M-Files Representative, Company: M-Files, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Provides metadata-driven document management systems to support faster business processes and AI readiness. Name: 1Fort AI Representative, Company: 1Fort AI, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Creates AI systems to reduce brokers’ manual work and increase client interaction time. Name: Ennabl Representative, Company: Ennabl, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Produces tools that automate administrative tasks by extracting and verifying data from documents and AMS systems.

item.title: The Readiness is All: New AIG CEO Andersen Talks Industry Relevance
item.description:
AIG appointed Eric Andersen as CEO on June 1, 2026, and he outlined the company’s focus on readiness for evolving risks, particularly emphasizing data center risks as a major opportunity that requires collaboration with alternative capital providers. Andersen described AIG’s use of AI to enhance underwriting and claims processes by automating data ingestion, improving risk modeling, and enabling underwriters to focus on higher-level analysis, while also educating new capital on risk parameters. This approach aims to improve underwriting efficiency and capacity, broaden risk-sharing with alternative capital, and maintain broker and insurer relevance amid technological changes.
Name: Eric Andersen, Company: AIG, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of AIG since June 2026 speaking on industry readiness and AI integration Name: Lawrence Wilkinson, Company: S&P Global Ratings, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Managing Director at S&P Global Ratings and moderator of the CEO Insights session

item.title: Cyber, AI and economic pressures dominate insurance concerns in RiskScan 2026
item.description:
RiskScan 2026 is a joint research study by the Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I) and Munich Re US based on a survey of more than 1,700 respondents, identifying cyber incidents, economic pressures, and artificial intelligence as the top insurance concerns. The study uses online survey data from U.S. and U.K. markets to analyze the convergence of cyber, economic, technological, and catastrophe risks and their systemic interconnections. These findings affect commercial underwriting by highlighting the need for updated cyber coverage, new catastrophe models, and AI-related product development, influencing insurer risk management and capacity allocation.
Name: Marcus Winter, Company: Munich Re US, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President and CEO North America (P&C Re) at Munich Re US Name: Michel Léonard, Ph.D., CBE, Company: Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief economist and data scientist at Triple-I Name: Sean Kevelighan, Company: Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I), Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of Triple-I Name: Kerri Hamm, Company: Munich Re US, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of Cyber Underwriting, Client Solutions, and Business Development at Munich Re US Name: Kane Wells, Company: Reinsurance News, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article and member of Reinsurance News team

item.title: Lemonade brings renters insurance to North Dakota
item.description:
Lemonade, a technology-driven insurance provider with over three million active customers, has launched its renters insurance product in North Dakota. The product operates through Lemonade’s app, enabling users to obtain quotes, purchase, update policies, and file claims, with about 40% of claims resolved instantly. This expansion offers North Dakota renters access to lower-priced renters insurance, which at about 30% below the national average, could pressure traditional carriers’ market share and enhance underwriting efficiency through digital automation.
Name: Dan Timsit, Company: Lemonade, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of renters insurance at Lemonade who commented on the company’s expansion into North Dakota

item.title: How Digit is uing AI-led claims engine to speed up insurance settlements
item.description:
Digit Insurance has implemented an AI-led, event-driven claims processing system that achieves near real-time settlements with specific performance metrics such as 60% of health insurance cashless hospitalizations approved within 20 minutes and 71% of motor repair approvals processed within 12 hours. The system automates document intake, classification, validation, and parallel decision-making steps using cloud-native technology and machine learning confidence models that automatically approve straightforward claims while routing complex cases to human reviewers. This approach improves operational efficiency, reduces manual workloads, enhances underwriting accuracy, and supports faster settlements that positively impact customer retention and claims leakage reduction.
Name: Vishal Shah, Company: Digit Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of Data Science at Digit Insurance responsible for the AI-led claims architecture and automation strategy

item.title: Specialty Insurance Market Expected to Reach $361.64 Billion by 2035
item.description:
The global specialty insurance market was valued at $110.39 billion in 2025 and is forecast to reach $361.64 billion by 2035, growing at a 12.73% CAGR. This growth is driven by increased cyber risks and climate-related catastrophes, supported by digital distribution platforms and data-driven underwriting models, including recent AI-native underwriting tools integrating cloud platforms. The expansion affects broker distribution share, with brokers controlling 58.61% currently, while insurtech and digital channels grow rapidly, improving underwriting speed and broadening capacity for emerging risks.
Name: Lloyd’s of London, Company: Lloyd’s of London, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Global insurance and reinsurance marketplace. Name: Google Cloud, Company: Google Cloud, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Cloud computing services provider collaborating on AI-native underwriting platforms. Name: AXA XL, Company: AXA XL, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Specialty insurance provider expanding products in nature-based risk and biodiversity liability aligned with EU frameworks. Name: CNPC, Company: CNPC, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Chinese state-owned petroleum corporation contributing to Asia Pacific insurance demand. Name: CNOOC, Company: CNOOC, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Chinese state-owned oil company involved in offshore drilling influencing regional risk exposure. Name: Sinopec, Company: Sinopec, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Chinese oil and gas enterprise impacting insurance needs in the Asia Pacific region.

item.title: VisitorsCoverage Launches Treppy MCP Server to Power AI-Driven Travel Insurance
item.description:
VisitorsCoverage Inc. launched the Treppy MCP server, a standardized AI integration platform enabling real-time travel insurance quotes from multiple carriers via a single endpoint. The server supports AI agents compliant with the Anthropic MCP standard, providing up-to-date plan details and seamless quote-to-compare workflows within AI booking and concierge systems. This technology reduces integration complexity, improving underwriting efficiency and enabling brokers and carriers to serve AI-driven purchase channels with lower operational friction.
Name: Rajeev Shrivastava, Company: VisitorsCoverage Inc., Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of VisitorsCoverage Inc.

item.title: ReSource Pro Releases Original Four-Part Research Series to Help Insurance Agencies Navigate a Tech Landscape Transformed by AI
item.description:
ReSource Pro released the Agency Tech Blueprint, a four-part research series designed to help retail insurance agencies evaluate and implement technology in an AI-driven environment. The series uses a three-level maturity model categorizing agency technology into foundational, optimizing, and transformational systems to guide investment and operational improvements. This framework aims to improve broker operational efficiency and supports agencies in adopting AI to enhance underwriting and client service without increasing risk.
Name: Mark Breading, Company: ReSource Pro, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Partner leading ReSource Pro Insights research and advisory. Name: Dan Epstein Tagger, Company: ReSource Pro, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO of ReSource Pro discussing AI adoption challenges in insurance. Name: Jared Kreiner, Company: ReSource Pro, Email: jared_kreiner@resourcepro.com, Type: Individual, Info: Contact person for ReSource Pro news releases. Name: Su-Ting Fu, Company: ReSource Pro, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Vice President, Data Strategy at ReSource Pro responsible for enterprise data and AI capability development.

item.title: ZestyAI Signs Up Adaptive Insurance -
item.description:
ZestyAI announced that Adaptive Insurance has integrated its Z-STORM™ model into underwriting and pricing for wind and hail coverage. Z-STORM™ uses AI trained on verified carrier claims data and local climatology combined with detailed property attributes to provide property-level severe storm risk scores. This integration allows Adaptive Insurance to price and underwrite storm risks more precisely, improving loss prediction and underwriting efficiency.
Name: Mike Gulla, Company: Adaptive Insurance, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO and Co-Founder of Adaptive Insurance Name: Attila Toth, Company: ZestyAI, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Founder and CEO of ZestyAI

item.title: Inside the reinvention of insurance
item.description:
Microsoft is advancing the use of AI agents across the insurance value chain to support underwriting, claims, and actuarial functions, enabling continuous engagement and early intervention. These AI agents process unstructured data such as medical records, satellite imagery, and sensor feeds to generate structured insights, automate workflows, and assist human experts under regulated oversight. This technology improves underwriting capacity, accelerates claims processing, reduces liability assessment times, and shifts insurance from reactive transactions toward proactive prevention.
Name: Jeffery Williams, Company: Microsoft, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Managing director in Microsoft’s insurance advisory organisation focusing on AI agents in insurance. Name: Will Hyams, Company: Howden Group Holdings, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Insurance executive discussing AI-driven real-time data for prevention. Name: Tyler Jones, Company: Duck Creek Technologies, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief marketing officer describing AI integration in insurance core platforms. Name: Jim DeMarco, Company: Microsoft, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of insurance advisory for worldwide financial services at Microsoft. Name: Legal-i, Company: Legal-i, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Swiss insurtech startup offering AI platforms for underwriting and claims assessments. Name: Alice Chambers, Company: Technology Record, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article on AI in insurance. Name: Sedgwick, Company: Sedgwick, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: Claims management company collaborating with Microsoft on AI solutions. Name: Cognizant, Company: Cognizant, Email: N/A, Type: Company, Info: IT services company providing AI governance and implementation frameworks for insurers.

item.title: Irish InsurTech Kayna Joins Guidewire InsurTech Vanguards Program
item.description:
Kayna, an embedded insurance distribution platform, announced it has joined the Guidewire Insurtech Vanguards program led by P&C cloud provider Guidewire. Kayna integrates vertical SaaS platforms with carriers, MGAs, and brokers to deliver data-driven, tailored insurance products to small and medium-sized businesses using business data within the SaaS environment. This integration creates a new, efficient distribution channel that improves carrier access to underserved SME markets and enhances broker and carrier revenue potential.
Name: Paul Prendergast, Company: Kayna, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: CEO and Co-Founder of Kayna, an embedded insurance platform connecting SaaS platforms with insurers. Name: Laura Drabik, Company: Guidewire, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Chief Evangelist at Guidewire, a cloud platform provider for P&C insurance.

item.title: Aon reports soft cyber and tech E&O insurance market amid growing risk challenges
item.description:
Aon reported in June 2026 that the cyber and technology errors and omissions (E&O) insurance market remains soft with abundant capacity and competitive pricing, despite increasing cyber risk complexities. The company’s analysis incorporates data on ransomware loss sizes, incident frequencies, AI-driven attack methods, supplier risk assessments, and evolving regulatory compliance, supported by enhanced underwriting scrutiny of cyber maturity and privacy practices. This environment sustains buyer-favorable margins and stable premium levels but pressures insurers to improve underwriting discipline and adapt coverage to emerging cyber and AI-related exposures.
Name: Matt Chmel, Company: Aon, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Head of Cyber Solutions, North America at Aon Name: Greg Sparacio, Company: Aon, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: US Middle Markets Leader at Aon Name: Stephen Viña, Company: Aon, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Vice President, Cyber Solutions at Aon North America Name: Ady Sharma, Company: Aon, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Cyber Growth Leader at Aon in Canada Name: Taylor Mixides, Company: Reinsurance News, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Author of the article and reporter at Reinsurance News

item.title: Canadians say they’re cautious about AI, but many are already letting it decide - insurance-canada.ca
item.description:
EY released findings from its 2026 AI Sentiment Study revealing that 13% of Canadians are using autonomous AI systems that act on their behalf, with widespread AI integration in sectors like energy, mobility, and customer experience. The study is based on survey data showing AI adoption linked to conditional trust built through user experience in everyday, low-risk decisions. This trend signals changing risk management dynamics that could influence commercial underwriting by increasing reliance on AI-driven decision-making while highlighting persistent security and accountability concerns impacting insurer and broker risk assessments.
Name: Biren Agnihotri, Company: EY Canada, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: EY Canada Chief Technology Officer and spokesperson on AI trust trends

item.title: AI adoption in finance doubles, but assurance readiness determines who wins, KPMG finds
item.description:
KPMG International released its 2026 Global AI in Finance survey analyzing AI adoption in finance functions at 1,013 senior leaders across 20 countries and 13 sectors, showing AI use doubled from 30 to 75 percent since 2024. The survey found organizations with assurance-ready AI—those with controls, governance, and auditability—achieve three to six times higher error reduction and greater confidence in scaling AI, while data quality remains the primary barrier and opportunity. These findings indicate stronger AI governance improves underwriting accuracy and operational efficiency, potentially raising broker margins and carrier capacity through more reliable AI-driven decisions.
Name: Sebastian Stöckle, Company: KPMG International, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Global Head of Audit Innovation & AI at KPMG International

item.title: ScienceSoft to Present AI Workbench for Claims Handling at Insurance Tech 2026
item.description:
ScienceSoft will present an AI claims workbench solution at the Insurance Tech and Innovation Conference on June 11, 2026, demonstrating AI-driven coordination of the claims lifecycle. The technology integrates with existing policy, claims, document, and communication systems using multiple AI models, including computer vision for photo analysis and decision logic with regulatory controls such as audit trails and human approval. This approach aims to reduce claims cycle times from weeks to same-day, improving carrier underwriting efficiency and enabling commercial brokers to better manage claims processes.
Name: Vital Soupel, Company: ScienceSoft, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Senior Insurance IT and AI Consultant at ScienceSoft

item.title: Hub International Appoints Vice President Of AI Sales Innovation
item.description:
Hub International Limited appointed Faizal Mitha as Vice President of AI Sales Innovation, starting July 1, 2026, to scale AI sales practices across multiple business lines in the U.S. and Canada. Mitha will expand the use of Anthropic’s Claude AI platform and develop tools, training, and tracking systems to enhance new business generation and organic growth. This role aims to improve producer efficiency and client engagement, potentially increasing commercial broker margins and supporting capacity growth through AI-driven insights.
Name: Faizal Mitha, Company: Hub International Limited, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Newly appointed Vice President of AI Sales Innovation at Hub International with over 20 years of digital and employee benefits leadership experience Name: Marc Cohen, Company: Hub International Limited, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: President and CEO of Hub International Limited

item.title: ChatGPT Review 2026: Is It Still the Best AI Chatbot?
item.description:
ChatGPT, developed by OpenAI, is the most widely used AI chatbot in 2026 with over 900 million weekly active users and multiple subscription plans ranging from free to enterprise levels. It leverages large language models (GPT-5.3, GPT-5.5, GPT-5.5 Pro) and integrates with external tools like Google Drive and Wolfram Alpha, offering features such as image generation, agent mode, advanced voice, and custom GPTs via API and subscription tiers. This platform improves commercial workflows by enabling faster content creation, coding assistance, and research summarization while paid plans increase usage limits and access to premium capabilities, enhancing broker productivity and reducing reliance on multiple AI tools.
Name: Mabel Kenneth, Company: OpenAI, Email: N/A, Type: Individual, Info: Content strategist and writer specializing in AI and emerging technology who authored the article.
